_meta.description: Source manifest for the Card Gyani MITC scraper. One entry per issuer. The scraper reads this file, downloads each MITC, extracts text/tables, and parses cards + bank-wide fee defaults. See scraper/README.md for the full spec.
important_notes: MITCs reliably give: card NAMES + annual/renewal fee + waiver condition, plus bank-wide terms (forex, finance %, cash advance, late-fee tiers).
MITCs usually DO NOT contain: rewards rate, lounge access, welcome benefits, category. Those come from the enrichment stage (product pages).
'format' tells the fetcher how to download. 'likely_scanned' is only a hint - the scraper must AUTO-DETECT scanned PDFs (if extracted text is near-empty, OCR it) regardless of this flag.

'product_listing_url' values are best-effort starting points for the enrichment stage and MUST be verified to resolve before use; if one 404s, have Claude Code search for the bank's current credit-cards page.

'card_coverage' describes how many cards the MITC enumerates: 'fee_table_all' = a big table listing many cards (richest), 'segment' = covers a product family, 'single_or_generic' = one card or generic terms only.

banks.notes: Large multi-page text PDF. Look for the fee schedule/annexure that lists card variants with annual + renewal fees.
Text PDF. Card-wise fee table expected.

HTML page (NOT a PDF). Contains a single fee table listing ~90 card variants with annual + renewal fee + waiver. Parse the HTML table directly. Bank-wide terms: finance up to 3.75%/mo unsecured (2.75%/mo secured), forex 3.5% (with named exceptions), late-fee tiers, cash advance 2.5% (min Rs.500).
Text PDF. Card-wise fee table expected.
Filename 'rcas' = Rates, Charges And Schedules. Likely the consolidated fee schedule across cards.
Text PDF. May state generic terms + per-card variations; confirm whether all variants are listed.

Dated 2016 ('Premium' segment) - OLD and may be image-based/scanned. AUTO-DETECT and OCR if text extraction is sparse. Also flag for review: this MITC may be stale; check the bank site for a newer consolidated MITC during enrichment.
BOBCARD (the BoB card subsidiary) calls it MITS, not MITC. Text PDF expected with card-wise fees.

This URL is the CHARGE-card MITC only. Amex also has credit cards with a separate MITC. During enrichment, look for the credit-card MITC too (e.g. .../mitc/credit-english-mitc.pdf) and add it as a second Amex source if found.
HTML landing page. It may link out to per-card MITC PDFs rather than holding the data inline - if so, follow those links and add them as child sources.
PDF served via a query-string endpoint (?name=...). The fetcher must follow redirects and may need a browser-like User-Agent; save the response as a .pdf.
Text PDF (URL has %20-encoded spaces). Card-wise fee table expected.
This is a T&C for SBM's SECURED (FD-linked) credit card - likely 1-2 cards only. Tag any card from here as type='secured' (FD-linked). URL contains an '&' that must be URL-encoded when fetching.
